children.text: the Secretary and the Director shall jointly issue guidance to all agencies directing the agencies to apply the data standards established under section 6402(a) to all applicable reporting by recipients of Federal awards; and
the Director shall prescribe guidance applying the data standards established under section 6402(a) to audit-related information reported under chapter 75 of this title.

children.label: A
children.text: minimize the disruption of existing reporting practices of, and not increase the reporting burden on, agencies or recipients of Federal awards; and

children.label: B
children.text: explore opportunities to implement modern technologies in reporting relating to Federal awards;

allow the Director to permit exceptions for classes of Federal awards, including exceptions for Federal awards granted to Indian Tribes and Tribal organizations consistent with the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act ( et seq.), if the Director publishes a list of those exceptions and submits the list to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs of the Senate and the Committee on Oversight and Reform of the House of Representatives; and25 U.S.C. 5301
take into consideration the consultation required under section 6402(d).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Not less frequently than once every 10 years, the Director shall update the guidance issued under subsection (a).

children.heading: .—Procedures
children.label: 2 .—Procedures
children.text: In updating guidance under paragraph (1), the Director shall, to the maximum extent practicable, follow the procedures for the development of the data standards and guidance prescribed under this section and section 6402.
